title: Stop percent-encoding the colon in /terminal/<pane_id> URLs
action: In pathForRoute (web/src/main.ts), unescape the ':' that encodeURIComponent produces (%3A) when building /terminal/<pane_id> so a pane_id like 'w5:p1Z' renders as /terminal/w5:p1Z instead of /terminal/w5%3Ap1Z. Every other character stays escaped as-is (in particular '/' still becomes %2F, so parseTerminalPaneId's [^/]+ segment match and its decodeURIComponent round-trip are unaffected -- decodeURIComponent already passes an unescaped ':' through unchanged). Update web/test/main.test.ts's pathForRoute assertion (currently expects '/terminal/w1%3Ap1') to expect the clean colon, and add/keep a parseTerminalPaneId round-trip case against the new clean form.

truths: pathForRoute({name:'terminal', agent}) with pane_id 'w1:p1' returns '/terminal/w1:p1', not '/terminal/w1%3Ap1'
A pane_id containing '/' still produces a URL where parseTerminalPaneId cannot misparse it (existing %2F escaping preserved)

artifacts.substantive: pathForRoute unescapes only ':' after encodeURIComponent, no other behavior change
pathForRoute/parseTerminalPaneId assertions updated to the clean-colon form

key_links: pathForRoute output must stay parseable by parseTerminalPaneId's TERMINAL_PATH_RE + decodeURIComponent
prohibitions: No change to parseTerminalPaneId's matching/decoding logic itself
No change to any other route's URL (login/switcher stay '/')

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: pathForRoute no longer percent-encodes ':' in /terminal/<pane_id> URLs (e.g. /terminal/w5:p1Z instead of /terminal/w5%3Ap1Z); '/' and everything else stay escaped, parseTerminalPaneId's round-trip unaffected.
